title: T-081 — Technical release adapter for NPD T-097 canonical release model
prompt:
# T-081 — Technical release adapter for NPD T-097 canonical release model

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5.1A, §7.4, prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md §0A
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Technical adapters for the NPD-owned canonical release model; no competing Technical release model.

NPD T-097 is canonical. This task imports/uses that model and adds Technical adapter functions for factory_specs and shared BOM bundle approval only. If the canonical implementation is not yet present, stop with a clear blocker referencing 01-npd/T-097 rather than inventing a fallback enum.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- apps/web/lib/technical/release-state-adapters.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/technical/factory-spec-release-guards.ts [create]
- tests/lib/technical/release-state-adapters.test.ts [create]
- tests/lib/technical/factory-spec-release-guards.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given canonical NPD T-097 release model exists, Technical adapters import/use it and no duplicate Technical/shared release enum is introduced.
2. Given factory_spec is created from NPD Builder, adapter maps initial canonical status to `in_review` and visible pending factory availability.
3. Given Technical approves factory_spec+BOM bundle, adapter maps canonical status to factory-usable approved/released UI state without changing D365 sync status.
4. Given released/approved_for_factory record receives direct update attempt, guard returns RELEASED_RECORD_IMMUTABLE and requires clone-on-write.
5. Given UI asks for badge metadata, adapter returns stable label, color token, allowed actions and blocking reason codes.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for adapter import/mapping to NPD T-097 canonical model.
- RED tests for illegal transitions, clone-on-write and D365 independence.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/lib/technical/release-state-adapters.test.ts tests/lib/technical/factory-spec-release-guards.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not create a competing release enum/model in Technical.
- Do not conflate local release status with D365 sync status.
- Do not treat NPD G4 as factory-use approval; Technical approval remains required.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
